reasoning: The Lean statement matches the informal one closely. `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin k)` is Mathlib's standard encoding of ℝ^k, `¬ Set.Countable E` correctly captures "E is uncountable", and `P = {x | ∀ U ∈ 𝓝 x, ¬ Set.Countable (U ∩ E)}` is exactly Rudin's definition of the set of condensation points of E (every neighborhood of x contains uncountably many points of E, i.e. U ∩ E is uncountable). The conclusion `Perfect P` uses Mathlib's `Perfect` structure (closed + every point is an accumulation point of itself), which is the standard definition. The quantifier structure, types, and ambient space all align with the informal claim.
